thesis: Testing the new comparison and newspaper scenes.

scenes.narration: In the mid-twentieth century, two global superpowers emerged from the ashes of World War Two. On one side stood the United States, championing capitalism. On the other, the Soviet Union, driven by a strict command economy. The stage was set for a conflict unlike any other.

visuals.directive: {"title": "The Cold War Divide", "left": {"title": "United States", "subtitle": "Capitalism", "triggerWord": "states", "points": [{"text": "Free Market", "triggerWord": "capitalism"}, {"text": "NATO Alliance", "triggerWord": "states"}]}, "right": {"title": "Soviet Union", "subtitle": "Communism", "triggerWord": "soviet", "points": [{"text": "Command Economy", "triggerWord": "economy"}, {"text": "Warsaw Pact", "triggerWord": "conflict"}]}}

scenes.narration: The tension spread rapidly across the ocean. From Moscow in the heart of the Soviet Union, to Washington D.C., the world was connected by a web of geopolitical chess.

The battleground wasn't just ideological; it was extraterrestrial. In 1969, the United States achieved the impossible. A human being set foot on another celestial body, sending shockwaves through the Soviet command and headlines across the globe.

visuals.directive: {"paperName": "THE GLOBAL TIMES", "date": "JULY 21, 1969", "headline": "MAN WALKS ON MOON!", "subhead": "Armstrong Takes Giant Leap For Mankind"}

scenes.narration: Declassified files later revealed the true extent of the operation. Operatives were secretly instructed to maintain surveillance on all key facilities.

visuals.directive: {"title": "CENTRAL INTELLIGENCE AGENCY", "date": "12 AUGUST 1969", "classification": "TOP SECRET", "refNumber": "CIA-RDP79-00927A004300080001-2", "text": "The subject has been observed making contact with known operatives in the sector. Further surveillance is required to determine the extent of the breach.", "highlights": [{"match": "making contact with known operatives", "triggerWord": "operatives"}, {"match": "surveillance is required", "triggerWord": "surveillance"}], "signature": "Director of Intelligence"}

scenes.narration: The world held its breath for thirteen days. Thirteen days that would determine whether humanity survived or perished in nuclear fire.
The toll was staggering. Over sixty million casualties worldwide. The economic cost exceeded four trillion dollars. And more than seventy million people were displaced from their homes.
Among history's deadliest conflicts, the Second World War stands alone with seventy million dead. The Mongol conquests claimed forty million. And the First World War took twenty million lives.

visuals.directive: {"title": "Deadliest Conflicts in History", "items": [{"rank": 1, "label": "World War II", "value": "70M", "triggerWord": "second"}, {"rank": 2, "label": "Mongol Conquests", "value": "40M", "triggerWord": "mongol"}, {"rank": 3, "label": "World War I", "value": "20M", "triggerWord": "first"}]}

scenes.narration: On July sixteenth, nineteen sixty nine, humanity took its greatest leap. The Apollo eleven mission lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, carrying three men to the moon.
Fast forward a century, and the world had transformed entirely. Sprawling megalopolises dominated the landscape, bathed in the glow of neon rain.
